name: Ranged Attack
description: An attack that applies damage or effects from a distance through projectiles, traces, or targeting rules.
design_notes: Ranged attacks need rules for aiming, ammunition, projectile behavior, line of sight, hit confirmation, and cover. Decide whether the attack is hitscan, simulated projectile, lock-on, arced, or area-targeted; each choice changes readability and balance.
design_purpose: Let players project threat beyond melee reach and interact with distant targets.
Create spatial decisions around cover, reloads, aim commitment, and projectile travel.
Support different weapon identities through spread, recoil, charge, ammo, and impact effects.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable of controlling space and landing precise attacks at range.

parameters.name: projectile_spawn_offset
parameters.description: Spawn position relative to the firing actor.

parameters.name: aim_spread_degrees
parameters.description: Angular dispersion applied to each shot.

parameters.name: damage_falloff_start_distance
parameters.description: Distance where damage reduction begins if falloff is used.

edge_cases: Firing from inside walls or near cover should not spawn projectiles behind collision.
Very fast projectiles need continuous collision or ray steps to avoid tunneling.
Projectile ownership must handle reflection, friendly fire, despawn, and save/load boundaries.
Aim assist, lock-on, and manual aim should not fight over target selection.
common_bugs: Projectile visuals and damage traces use different origins, causing visible misses to hit.
Ammo is consumed before server rejection and not refunded after prediction correction.
Spread uses frame-random state and breaks deterministic replays or seeded runs.
Projectiles continue after scene unload because lifetime cleanup is tied to renderer visibility.
balancing_notes: Long reach needs counter-pressure such as reload, ammo, spread, travel, cover, or enemy mobility.
Projectile travel gives defenders readable counterplay; hitscan needs stronger aim or exposure constraints.
accessibility_notes: Offer reticle scaling, color options, and aim assist settings where precision aiming is central.
Use hit markers or impact effects so players can distinguish misses, armor hits, and blocked shots.

implementation_notes.unity: Model startup, active, recovery, and cancel windows as data, then drive hitboxes, animation events, and damage application from one combat state source. For Ranged Attack, validate this path with parameters such as projectile_spawn_offset and projectile_travel_units_per_second.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D hit volumes with frame-window state on the actor; avoid placing damage authority inside animation-only callbacks. For Ranged Attack, validate this path with parameters such as projectile_spawn_offset and projectile_travel_units_per_second.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement the action as a Gameplay Ability or Actor Component with montage notifies for windows and authoritative damage on the server when networked. For Ranged Attack, validate this path with parameters such as projectile_spawn_offset and projectile_travel_units_per_second.
implementation_notes.web: Update combat windows in the fixed simulation step, evaluate hurtbox overlap from cached transforms, and emit presentation events separately from damage state. For Ranged Attack, validate this path with parameters such as projectile_spawn_offset and projectile_travel_units_per_second.
